command_or_fixture: Control-flow trace and registered-wrapper inspection for wave_create_wave, wave_prepare, wave_review, and _lifecycle_context_result; replay of buffered-intent flush semantics
counterexample_or_failure_condition: A dry-run or incomplete review intent survives in the process buffer and is later flushed as durable workflow savings.
expected: Dry runs and incomplete implementation reviews may set focus if documented, but contribute no durable lifecycle credit; only completed mutating milestones and completed review gates are creditable.
known_bad_detection_method: The review traced the exact accepted statuses, intent buffering, later mutating flush, and absence of a review-completion predicate across the registered wrappers.

proposition: Durable lifecycle-efficiency credit must be awarded only for a completed lifecycle milestone, independently of whether a call establishes wave focus.
public_path: Registered wave_create_wave, wave_prepare, wave_review, wave_implement, and wave_close telemetry hooks

disposition_rationale: Focus association may legitimately include dry runs, but durable token-savings credit must represent completed lifecycle milestones. The current code conflates association with credit and can publish savings for work that did not complete. Separate the two predicates and add focused lifecycle regressions.

command_or_fixture: Trace registered wave_garden(mode=run) through run_garden/docs_gardener write_text and compare with telemetry checkpoint projection under review_event_write_lock; construct the two-writer interleaving
